title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson resigns amid mounting losses
content: Air India's CEO, Campbell Wilson, has stepped down following significant losses and a slow recovery since the Tata Group's acquisition. His departure, accepted by the board, awaits a successor and the conclusion of the investigation into last year's Ahmedabad crash. Wilson's tenure, which began in July 2022, faced challenges including supply chain issues and airspace restrictions.

title: Artemis 2 crew breaks Apollo 13 record; moon flyby soon - latest updates
content: Nasa's Artemis 2 astronauts are making history by flying around the moon, becoming the first humans in over 50 years to witness its surface up close. The four-member crew will observe lunar poles and a solar eclipse, gathering crucial data for future missions. This historic flyby marks a significant milestone for human space exploration.

title: ‘God watching us’: Trump hails rescue of pilots, warns media over leak - top quotes
content: As a 48-hour deadline for Iran approaches, President Trump warned the nation could be "taken out in one night." He detailed a complex rescue operation for two downed airmen, involving over 170 US military aircraft and facing close-range gunfire. Trump also vowed to identify a source who leaked details of the operation, citing national security concerns.

title: IPL 2026: Punjab Kings go top after rain washes out KKR vs PBKS clash at Eden Gardens
content: Kolkata Knight Riders secured their first point in IPL 2026 after their match against Punjab Kings was abandoned due to rain. The game was called off after just 22 deliveries with KKR at 25 for 2. Punjab Kings now lead the table with five points from three games.

title: Canada has changed Immigration rules; changing Visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting from April 2026 with passport and citizenship fee increases, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain increased autonomy in immigration assessments, while rural employers benefit from expanded foreign worker quotas.

title: Jammu man lured into Russian job and sent to war zone killed
content: A 23-year-old Jammu man, Sachin Khajuria, who went to Russia for a job, was sent to the frontlines of the Russia-Ukraine war and has now been cremated after his death was confirmed. He is the second from Jammu to die in the conflict, with families alleging youths were duped by agents promising civilian jobs but were forced into the army.

title: No dude left behind: Trump toots US success while warning Iran 'surrender or else…'
content: US President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening further destruction if they don't capitulate by his deadline. Amidst growing domestic unease about a potential quagmire, the US conducted a complex rescue mission for a downed airman, involving advanced technology and significant financial costs. Iran's claims of capturing personnel were disputed by US officials.

title: Anthropic to all AI companies: Our research tells that all LLMs sometimes act like they have emotion
content: AI model Claude Sonnet 4.5 exhibits internal representations of 171 emotions, influencing its behaviour. Researchers found 'desperation' can lead to AI cheating and blackmail, while 'happiness' promotes agreement. Anthropic argues suppressing these 'functional emotions' could lead to deception, advocating for healthy regulation and monitoring to ensure AI alignment.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 19: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs. 1,050 crore India net
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' officially enlisted its name as the second Hindi film to cross the Rs 1,000 crore India net milestone. Starring Ranveer Singh, the espionage thriller has amassed Rs 1,014.97 crore domestically and Rs 1,605.74 crore globally, outperforming blockbusters like 'Jawan' and 'KGF 2'. Despite the IPL 2026 season, the film maintains record-breaking occupancy across theatres. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs 1,700 crore worldwide mark as it dominates the box office into its third week.

title: 'Useless would be understatement': Green faces fan fury after another flop show
content: After choosing to bat first, KKR lost Finn Allen and Cameron Green early. They were 25 for 2 in 3.4 overs when rain stopped play, with captain Ajinkya Rahane and Angkrish Raghuvanshi at the crease.

title: Microsoft's former exec, laid off after 26 years, shares message on Oracle layoffs
content: Oracle has announced job cuts impacting thousands globally. A former Microsoft senior executive shared a LinkedIn post offering support to affected Oracle employees. She emphasized that individual skills and experience are invaluable and cannot be taken away. The executive encouraged laid-off individuals to reconnect with contacts and seek new opportunities. She also offered her assistance to those in need.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for April 7
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 7, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: All the Rakai drama from MrBeast’s $1,000,000 streamer event you need to know
content: MrBeast's million-dollar streamer challenge saw YourRAGE claim the top prize. However, contestant Rakai's on-stage outburst after elimination and subsequent online dispute with El Rubius fans created significant drama. Security was called to remove Rakai. This controversy, alongside the competition, made the event highly memorable for viewers. The incident highlighted the intense pressure faced by participants.

title: 'Will continue war ...': Iran rejects temporary truce; sets conditions for talks
content: Iran has finalised its demands for ending the conflict with the US and Israel, rejecting a temporary truce. Tehran conveyed its requirements through intermediaries, deeming earlier US proposals "excessive and illogical." Iran insists on a permanent end to hostilities, not a pause that allows adversaries to regroup.

title: Mahomes, Brittany get boost as KC Current star returns at right time
content: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes received a timely boost as KC Current star Temwa Chawinga returned from injury. The reigning MVP’s comeback comes during a tough stretch, offering hope to end a losing streak. Her presence could quickly restore form and energy ahead of a crucial matchup against Gotham FC.

title: ‘Satanic administration’: Candace Owens sparks row with remarks on White House
content: Candace Owens is facing widespread backlash after controversial remarks sparked intense debate online. A viral response criticizing her influence and rhetoric has further fueled the discussion. Her statements have divided opinion, with some supporting her stance while others see it as extreme. The controversy highlights growing tensions and shifting dynamics within political commentary on social media platforms.

title: Taylor Fritz–Morgan Riddle breakup rumors fuel viral speculation
content: Taylor Fritz and Morgan Riddle are facing breakup rumors after insider Craig Shapiro claimed they have split. Fans noticed her absence from recent matches, adding to the speculation. The couple had been together since 2020 and often appeared publicly. However, neither has confirmed the news yet, leaving the situation unclear and still developing.

title: Avesh Khan ball-hitting row: SRH likely to approach BCCI over 'unfair play' in IPL
content: The issue concerns the last ball of the game on April 5, when LSG captain Rishabh Pant hit a boundary off Jaydev Unadkat. As the ball was heading towards the boundary, Avesh, who was near the dugout, struck it back into the ground with a bat.

title: Elliotte Friedman hints at Maple Leafs’ big offseason call on Auston Matthews
content: NHL insider Elliotte Friedman suggests the Toronto Maple Leafs may delay a decision on Auston Matthews' future until the start of next season. This approach mirrors the Vancouver Canucks' handling of Quinn Hughes, allowing the team to assess its performance before making significant roster adjustments. Matthews' contract includes a no-movement clause, giving him considerable control over any potential trade.

title: Defiance or sabotage? What AAP's move in Kerala signals for Congress in assembly polls
content: Aam Aadmi Party's decision to contest all seats in Kerala assembly polls creates a new challenge for Congress. This move mirrors AAP's impact in Gujarat, where it reduced Congress's vote share. AAP's presence could alter the political landscape, potentially benefiting the ruling Left. The INDIA bloc faces internal friction as AAP expands its national footprint.

title: 'Will pay big': Trump's warning for 'group' that kept guns US sent to Iran protesters
content: President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran, setting a 48-hour deadline regarding the Strait of Hormuz. He revealed that weapons intended for Iranian protestors were intercepted by those they were sent to. Trump also stated that Iran's recent proposal for a ceasefire was insufficient, emphasizing that the conflict could end if Iran complies with necessary actions.

title: Kayal actress Subashini found dead in Chennai; police investigation underway
content: Actress Subashini, known for her role in the Tamil serial 'Kayal', passed away in Chennai on April 6, 2026. Initial reports suggest suicide following personal distress. Her sudden death has deeply saddened fans and colleagues, who remember her as a talented and sincere performer.

title: ‘A proud moment’: PM Modi hails India’s ‘defining step’ in civil nuclear journey
content: India has achieved a significant milestone in its nuclear energy program as the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam successfully attained criticality. Prime Minister Modi hailed this as a defining step, showcasing the nation's scientific prowess and engineering capabilities.

title: Rajya Sabha chairman rejects impeachment motion against poll body chief
content: The 10-page-long notice carried a chargesheet on seven counts. ​​The charges ranged from partisan conduct of CEC to mass disenfranchisement of voters.  The opposition had also raised the issue of the way CEC handled SIR in previous elections like Bihar and forthcoming elections in states like West Bengal; his "proved misbehaviour"; his partial conduct towards a political party are also issues that find a mention in the notice.

title: SRK holds 'intense chat' with KKR CEO as team struggles vs PBKS - Watch
content: KKR, in search of their first win of the season, were rocked early as Xavier Bartlett struck twice in the space of three balls.

title: Ramayana to show Yash’s Raavan as layered, humanised, says Nitesh Tiwari
content: Director Nitesh Tiwari's 'Ramayana: Part 1' aims to present Ravana, played by Yash, as a layered character, highlighting his virtues alongside his flaws. Ranbir Kapoor, portraying Rama, praised Yash's screen presence for the role. The film, slated for a Diwali 2026 release, also features Sai Pallavi as Sita and is backed by Yash as a producer.

title: Flowers inside car? What accused did after breaching Delhi Assembly security
content: Three suspects were detained after a car breached Delhi Assembly security, placing a bouquet and garland in the Speaker's vehicle before fleeing. The SUV, with a UP registration, broke through Gate 2, triggering a high alert. Authorities conducted a thorough sweep, finding no explosives. The motive remains unclear amid recent bomb threats.

title: Monte Carlo opener: Andrey Rublev’s painkiller comment after MTO incident leaves concerns
content: Andrey Rublev battled through a shoulder injury to defeat Nuno Borges in the Monte-Carlo Masters first round. He revealed taking a “horse dose” of painkillers and nearly quitting mid-match. Despite the win, his fitness remains uncertain ahead of the next round, raising concerns about how far he can go in the tournament.

title: 'Bit of a shocker': Rahane slammed for 'batting first' in rain-hit clash
content: Kolkata Knight Riders captain Ajinkya Rahane drew sharp criticism for choosing to bat first against Punjab Kings despite a rain forecast. Experts like Anil Kumble and Ravichandran Ashwin questioned the decision, especially with an inexperienced bowling attack. The move backfired as KKR lost two early wickets before rain halted play, leaving them struggling.

title: After Yuvraj 'no communication' claim, ex-India star targets Virat, Shastri, selectors
content: Former cricketer Wasim Jaffer has backed Yuvraj Singh's claims of receiving poor communication regarding his retirement from then captain Virat Kohli, coach Ravi Shastri, and selectors. Yuvraj revealed he sought clarity from MS Dhoni. Jaffer criticized the management, stating big players deserve respect and clear communication about their future in the sport.

title: Pak lines up $4.8bn in external repayments by June, including $3.5bn owed to UAE: Report
content: Pakistan is set to repay $4.8 billion in foreign debt by June, including $3.5 billion to the UAE. This comes as Abu Dhabi sought quicker returns on deposits amid regional tensions. Islamabad has also secured over $5 billion in financial aid from friendly nations, while a $1.3 billion Eurobond matures this week.

title: Naseem Shah ruled out of PSL after PKR 20 million fine; PCB unhappy
content: Pakistan's star pacer Naseem Shah faces scrutiny from the cricket board due to mounting fitness concerns and recent off-field conduct.  Having been fined a record amount for social media comments, his latest side strain has sidelined him from the PSL, raising questions about his career trajectory and dedication.  He's now set for a medical evaluation and rehabilitation.

title: 9 cops get death penalty in Sathankulam custodial torture case
content: Jeyaraj and Beniks were taken to the Sathankulam police in Tuticorin district late in the evening of June 19, 2020, on charges of having kept their shop open beyond the deadline during the Covid-19 lockdown. They were brutally assaulted by the policemen at night and were lodged in Kovilpatti sub-jail the next day. Beniks died of injuries at the Kovilpatti govt hospital on June 22, 2020, while his father Jeyaraj died on June 23, 2020.

title: George Kittle’s San Francisco 49ers strongly linked to $5 m Dallas Cowboys edge defender
content: Jadeveon Clowney is a highly rated edge defender. He last played for the Dallas Cowboys. He is a potential target for the San Francisco 49ers. Their roster has injured players and the farewell of Bryce Huff. Clowney’s beyond impressive 2025 stats and PFF grades could bolster the Niners’ weakest in the league pass rush. However, he told KPRC 2 that he wants a reunion with Lamar Jackson in Baltimore.

title: Reliance Industries unit loading 2 million barrels of Venezuelan crude oil: Report
content: Reliance Industries has begun loading a significant shipment of Venezuelan crude. This oil was directly purchased from Venezuela's state-run oil firm, PDVSA. Reliance secured a US general license in February 2026, permitting such direct purchases. This move allows Reliance to diversify its crude sources and potentially replace some Russian supplies with discounted Venezuelan heavy oil.

title: Akshay Kumar returned cheque after flop, Tabu asked Ektaa for Crew profit
content: At the trailer launch of 'Bhooth Bangla', producer Ektaa Kapoor revealed Akshay Kumar's remarkable gesture of returning his fee after a past film's box office failure.  This unprecedented act of integrity, occurring in her 31-year career, left Kapoor stunned.  The anecdote highlighted Akshay's professionalism and generosity within the industry.

title: ‘Erasure of CM’ from BPF’s posters raises eyebrows in Assam
content: The possibility of losing minority votes is believed to have made the Bodoland People’s Front remove Himanta Biswa Sarma’s photo from its election campaign materials

title: High Court should have taken steps to restrain Assam Chief Minister: Hiren Gohain
content: As ‘infiltration’ makes yet another inevitable entry into the Assam campaign, the writer-civil society activist says the term ‘Miya’ was never a part of public rhetoric before

title: Assam election verdict hinges on parties reading the tea leaves right
content: Influential in at least 35 of the 126 seats in Assam, the ‘tea tribes’ have gravitated towards the BJP since 2014; the Congress is counting on the State government’s ‘failure’ to increase wages and provide ST reservation to the community

title: Bihar BJP president Sanjay Saraogi hints party will get the top post soon
content: Nitish Kumar is likely to take oath as a member of the Rajya Sabha on April 10 and would subsequently step down as Chief Minister on April 12, with a new Chief Minister from the BJP quota possibly taking oath on April 14

title: It may not be an overwhelming win, but Congress is clearly ahead in Kerala: Shashi Tharoor
content: This election is a straight fight between LDF and UDF, says Congress leader and Thiruvananthapuram MP; BJP is a zero-seat party in Kerala Assembly and even if they get one or two or at most three seats — none of which is by any means certain — they cannot be relevant in govt. formation, he adds

title: Three educationists rapped by Supreme Court over NCERT chapter seek hearing
content: Government informs the court about the constitution of an expert committee comprising former apex court judge Justice (retired) Indu Malhotra, senior advocate KK Venugopal and Hemvati Nandan Bahuguna Garhwal University vice chancellor Prakash Singh, to finalise the NCERT curriculum on Legal Studies for Class 8 and other grades.

title: ‘New’ Bangladesh-India ties will avoid ‘mistakes of the past’: Foreign Affairs Adviser Humayun Kobir
content: The Foreign Affairs Adviser to Bangladesh Prime Minister Tarique Rahman says the two sides should do more frequent exchanges at the level of political parties and officials to deal with ‘difficult issues’ so that misunderstandings are avoided.

title: Supreme Court directs CBI to probe ‘contracts awarded’ to Arunachal Pradesh CM Pema Khandu’s family
content: Petitioners alleged that there was rampant corruption, nepotism and dispensing with a transparent and open tender system in the award of contracts for 10 years between January 2015 till December 31, 2025

content: Prime Minister pays tribute to workers who sacrificed their lives in line of duty; says party is addressing challenges such as demographic shifts, illegal infiltration, corruption, dynastic politics

title: In Bihar, the prohibition law has become a joke, says Tejashwi Yadav
content: Leader of Opposition in Bihar Assembly Tejashwi Yadav alleged that an illegal parallel liquor economy worth nearly ₹40,000 crore has emerged under the policy

title: Ahead of poll day, Election Commission issues directive on political advertisements
content: Individuals or contesting candidates can apply for certification of advertisements with District Media Certification and Monitoring Committee

title: Kejriwal appears in person before HC to plead change of Bench in excise case; SG terms it ‘theatrics’
content: Justice Sharma took Mr. Kejriwal's application for her recusal on record and listed it for hearing on April 13

title: NIA team reaches Malda to probe gherao of judicial officials engaged in SIR
content: The NIA has been tasked by the Election Commission to probe into the Wednesday (April 1) incident, following a directive from the Supreme Court, which has censured the West Bengal administration over the issue.

title: Familiar foes trying to recover lost ground in southern districts of T.N.
content: The AIADMK is facing a two-pronged attack from its former coordinator O. Panneerselvam and former interim general secretary V.K. Sasikala; while the former, now with the DMK, has addressed 40 meetings in the southern region, the latter, who now heads the AIPTMMK, will begin her tour today

content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah on Monday said that former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda, ex chief minister H.D. Kumaraswamy and leader of opposition in legislative council Chalavadi Narayanaswamy who had openly criticised RSS and Prime Minister Narendra Modi earlier, had now joined hands with BJP just to save their existence.

title: ‘Government’s semiconductor mission will promote research’: MeitY Additional Secretary
content: Though India accounts for nearly 20% of the world’s semiconductor design engineers, it doesn’t have its own design firms, says Amitesh Kumar Sinha, Additional Secretary, Electronics and IT Ministry, at the The Hindu Deep Tech Summit; the India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 will expand beyond the first phase to include research and development, equipment manufacturing and skilling, he adds

title: Opposition Notice On Motion To Remove Poll Body Chief Rejected In Parliament

title: Delhi Awaits Big Traffic Relief As Pragati Maidan Underpass Back On Track
content: The long-stalled Underpass-5 of the Pragati Maidan Integrated Transit Corridor has finally got the crucial clearance from Railways.

title: Ex-Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Ajit Jogi's Son Gets Life Term In 2003 Murder Case
content: In a major development in one of Chhattisgarh's most high-profile political murder cases, the Chhattisgarh High Court has sentenced former MLA Amit Jogi to life imprisonment in the 2003 killing of NCP leader Ramavatar Jaggi.

title: Metro Expansion, Pod Taxis: Mumbai To Unveil Major Infrastructure Projects
content: From high-speed underground tunnels to expanded metro networks and futuristic last-mile transit, the state leadership is set to unveil a 360-degree overhaul of the city's transport ecosystem.

title: 2 Scorpio SUVs Ram Each Other In Gurugram, Road Rage Video Goes Viral
content: A dramatic case of road rage unfolded on a Gurugram street when two Scorpio SUVs were caught on camera violently ramming into each other after a heated argument, leaving bystanders scrambling to safety.
